title: Boat Hire in Brac Island: A Budget Guide for 2026
html:
Exploring Brac Island by boat on a budget is straightforward when you know where to look. Renting a small motorboat from Bol harbour can cost as little as €150 for a half-day, while a shared tour from Supetar runs from €60 per person. BoatHire24 connects you with verified operators across the island, making it easy to find a deal that suits your wallet.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Brac Island?

Budget boat rental in Brac Island starts at €60 per person for group tours and rises to €1,200 per day for a crewed yacht. Prices vary by boat type, season, and duration. For a solo traveller or couple, a small motorboat without skipper offers the best value at around €150 for a half-day. A catamaran or yacht with skipper is ideal for groups and can be shared to lower the cost per person. Always check the best rates during the shoulder months of May and September, when demand is lower. You can yacht charter in Brac Island for your chosen dates.

Where to set off in Brac Island

Brac Island offers several departure points for boat hire. The main harbour is Bol, known for the famous Zlatni Rat beach and a range of boat rental offices. Supetar, the island’s largest town, has a ferry port and easy access to the nearby bay of Splitska. Milna, on the western coast, is a sheltered harbour popular with yachts. One option worth a look is the Salty Sun Charters charter page.

Other useful spots include Sumartin on the eastern side and Bobovišća, a small cove with several moorings. From any of these points you can reach the Pakleni Islands just a few nautical miles away, or sail south to the island of Hvar. For those interested in a full day trip, a visit to the Blue Cave on Vis is achievable from Milna or Bol in a fast boat. If you own a boat, you can become a host and earn from spare days.

Which boat to rent in Brac Island

  1. Small motorboat (4-6 guests), ideal for exploring coves near Bol or Supetar; half-day rates from €150.
  2. Catamaran (8-12 guests), spacious and stable for day trips to the Pakleni Islands; full-day hire from €700.
  3. Motor yacht (6-10 guests), a stylish option like the Salty Sun Charters yacht; check the Salty Sun Charters charter page for details; from €800 per day.
  4. Guided speedboat tour (up to 12 guests), includes visits to the Blue Cave and Hvar; from €90 per person.

Costs at a glance for Brac Island boat rental

  • Shared group tour: €60-€90 per person
  • Small motorboat half-day: €150-€250
  • Catamaran full-day: €700-€1,200
  • Bareboat yacht weekly: €1,500-€3,500
  • Crewed yacht (6 guests): from €1,200 per day

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Brac Island?

For motorboats over 15 hp or longer than 5 metres, a valid boating licence is required. The operator will confirm the exact requirements for your nationality before you book. Licensed skippers are available for hire if you do not hold one, which adds around €150 to the daily cost. The high season runs from June to September when the maestral wind picks up in the afternoon, making morning trips more comfortable for novices.

When anchoring near Zlatni Rat, remember to stay clear of the marked swim zones and drop the anchor on sand, not seagrass, to protect the marine environment.

At a glance

  • Price band: €60-€1,200 per day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Typical trip length: half-day (4 hours) to full-day (8 hours)
  • Maximum guests: from 4 on a small motorboat to 12 on a catamaran
  • Currency: EUR
  • Licence needed: yes, for most self-drive boats over 5m

Questions about boat rental in Brac Island

Can I rent a boat in Brac Island without a licence?

Yes, you can join a guided tour or hire a boat with a skipper, which is often included in the price for larger yachts.

Is it cheaper to rent a boat for a week than per day?

Weekly bareboat rates are generally lower per day than daily hire, especially for catamarans and yachts during the shoulder season.

What is the best time of day to go boating in Brac?

Morning trips from 9am to 1pm are popular because the sea is calmer and the maestral wind has not yet strengthened.

Can I visit the Blue Cave from Brac Island?

Yes, several speedboat tours depart from Milna and Bol, taking about 30 minutes to reach the cave entrance.

Booking with BoatHire24 gives you access to verified boat owners in Brac Island and beyond. Compare options for a small motorboat or a crewed catamaran, and plan your trip with confidence. If you own a boat in Brac, you can also list your vessel and earn extra income by becoming a host on BoatHire24.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Brac Island and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
